creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Your COPYTREE_SHARE make variable may be at fault. It is in Mk/bsd.port.mk at ~ 2184. It is responsible for copying the ldap and sql directories to /usr/local/share. Mine works. Yours does not. Below is what it should be.

COPYTREE_SHARE= ${SH} -c '(${FIND} -Ed $$1 $$3 | ${CPIO} -dumpl $$2 >/dev/null 2>&1) && \
                           ${FIND} -Ed $$1 $$3 \(   -type d -exec ${SH} -c '\''cd '\''$$2'\'' && chmod 755 "$$@"'\'' . {} + \
                                                 -o -type f -exec ${SH} -c '\''cd '\''$$2'\'' && chmod ${_SHAREMODE} "$$@"'\'' . {} + \)' COPYTREE_SHARE

The question is why does yours not work?

$1 is your sql subdirectory in $WRKSRC, $2 your ldap. Can you tar your $WRKSRC and attach, please. Either your cpio is faulty or, more likely, the sql and ldap directories are empty.
